nysed-ela-2022-g3-mc-q1.alt: Question 1. In paragraph 3, what is most likely the narrator’s point of view of Stephanie? Choices: A: Stephanie likes to talk with many people on the bus. B: Sometimes Stephanie acts younger than she really is. C: Stephanie likes to stay friends with people for a long time. D: Sometimes Stephanie acts in a mean way toward a friend.

nysed-ela-2022-g3-mc-q2.alt: Question 2. What does the phrase “left out” mean as used in paragraph 7? Choices: A: watched B: alone C: troubled D: careful

nysed-ela-2022-g3-mc-q3.alt: Question 3. Which quotation best shows how Rosa and Stephanie usually act with each other? Choices: A: “Rosa and Stephanie did everything together.” (paragraph 2) B: “She walked away to join Toni and Vanessa, and Rosa was left standing alone.” (paragraph 5) C: “Rosa tried not to check the mirror to see if Stephanie had noticed” (paragraph 7) D: “But Rosa walked past her and slid into the empty seat next to Kiara.” (paragraph 8)

nysed-ela-2022-g3-mc-q4.alt: Question 4. In paragraphs 16 through 20, the reader can see that Rosa changes from Choices: A: trying to avoid someone to making new friends B: feeling sad about something to having a good time C: wanting to upset her friend to wanting to include her D: having a good time to feeling angry at someone

nysed-ela-2022-g3-mc-q5.alt: Question 5. In paragraph 21, what does the phrase “Stephanie brightened” mean? Choices: A: She became smarter. B: She moved into the sunlight. C: She changed into dressy clothes. D: She became happier.

nysed-ela-2022-g3-mc-q6.alt: Question 6. Which sentence best supports the central message of the story? Choices: A: Stephanie makes room for Rosa to sit next to her on the bus. B: Stephanie tells Rosa that she is going to the pool with someone else. C: Rosa tells Stephanie that she has plans with Kiara. D: Rosa asks Stephanie if she wants to go to the pool with her and Kiara.

nysed-ela-2022-g3-mc-q7.alt: Question 7. What question does paragraph 3 help to answer? Choices: A: How do ears take in sounds? B: What do eardrums do? C: How do noises travel? D: What do sounds feel like?

nysed-ela-2022-g3-mc-q8.alt: Question 8. Which sentence explains why there are no sounds in outer space? Choices: A: Sound waves cannot travel fast enough to be heard in space. B: Sound waves cannot bounce off things in space. C: There is no air in space for sound waves to travel through. D: There are no people or animals in space to hear sound waves.

nysed-ela-2022-g3-mc-q9.alt: Question 9. What happens right after sound waves cause the eardrum to vibrate? Choices: A: The brain makes sense of what is heard. B: Nerve hairs send signals to the brain. C: Sound waves travel through the ear canal. D: Some sound waves cause lower sounds.

nysed-ela-2022-g3-mc-q10.alt: Question 10. Which phrase from the passage best explains the meaning of “silence” as it is used in paragraph 7? Choices: A: “There are no sounds” (paragraph 7) B: “Because there is no air” (paragraph 7) C: “sound waves bounce back” (paragraph 8) D: “clap your hands in an empty room” (paragraph 8)

nysed-ela-2022-g3-mc-q11.alt: Question 11. Which sentence best states the main idea of the passage? Choices: A: Some noises are loud and others are quiet. B: Sound waves do not travel through all things the same way. C: Vibrations create sound waves that let us hear noises. D: There is no air or sound in outer space.

nysed-ela-2022-g3-mc-q12.alt: Question 12. Based on the passage, what does the author most likely think about how people hear? Choices: A: Learning how people understand sounds is very interesting. B: It is strange that all sounds do not return to where they start. C: It is hard to find out how people make sense of sounds. D: The fact that there is no sound in space is a problem.
